name: Saul
note: First king of Israel; a 40-year reign per Acts 13:21

name: David
note: Seven years at Hebron, then thirty-three over all Israel at Jerusalem

name: Solomon
note: Built the temple; the kingdom divided at his death

name: Rehoboam
note: First king of the southern kingdom after the division

name: Jeroboam I
note: First king of the northern kingdom; set up the calves at Bethel and Dan

name: Ahab
note: Married Jezebel; opposed by Elijah

name: Hezekiah
note: Survived Sennacherib's 701 BC siege; a co-regency from c. 729 BC is debated

name: Josiah
note: Great reforms after the Book of the Law was found

name: Nebuchadnezzar II
note: Destroyed Jerusalem in 586 BC; the king of Daniel 1-4

name: Belshazzar
note: Co-regent for his father Nabonidus; slain the night Babylon fell

name: Cyrus the Great
note: Took Babylon in 539 BC; decreed the return in 538 BC

name: Darius the Mede
note: Ruler in Daniel 6; his precise identity is historically debated

name: Ahasuerus (Xerxes I)
note: The Persian king of the book of Esther

name: Samuel
note: Last judge and the prophet who anointed Saul and David

name: Nathan
note: Court prophet to David

name: Elijah
note: Confronted Ahab and the prophets of Baal

name: Elisha
note: Succeeded Elijah with a double portion of his spirit

name: Jonah
note: Sent to Nineveh during the reign of Jeroboam II

name: Amos
note: Judean shepherd who prophesied against the north

name: Hosea
note: His marriage a living sign to Israel

name: Isaiah
note: Ministry from Uzziah's death through Hezekiah

name: Jeremiah
note: The weeping prophet through Jerusalem's fall

name: Ezekiel
note: Prophesied among the exiles by the Chebar canal

name: Daniel
note: Served from Nebuchadnezzar to Cyrus

name: Haggai
note: Urged the returned exiles to rebuild the temple

name: Zechariah
note: Contemporary of Haggai; visions of restoration

name: Malachi
note: Final voice of Old Testament prophecy

name: John the Baptist
note: The forerunner; 'a voice crying in the wilderness'
